name: PagerDuty MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official PagerDuty MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with PagerDuty's incident management platform — querying incidents, managing on-call schedules, triggering alerts, acknowledging and resolving incidents, and accessing service health data.
use_cases: Querying active incidents and severity from incident response agents
Acknowledging and resolving incidents from automated remediation agents
Checking on-call schedules for escalation routing agents
Triggering alerts from monitoring and detection agents
Summarizing incident history for post-mortem analysis agents
Managing escalation policies from workflow automation agents
not_for: Teams using OpsGenie, VictorOps, or Rootly for incident management
Service monitoring setup (use Datadog, Prometheus for that)
best_when: An agent needs to query or manage incidents, on-call schedules, or service health in a PagerDuty-powered operations environment.
avoid_when: You're using OpsGenie, VictorOps, or another incident management platform.

auth.notes: PagerDuty API keys (user-level) or OAuth2 apps. API keys have read/write scope per resource type. Service accounts recommended for agents.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 5 users, basic incident management
pricing.notes: Per-seat pricing. Free tier available. Enterprise customers often have custom plans. MCP server is open source.

known_agent_gotchas: Service IDs and escalation policy IDs required — discover these before triggering alerts
Incident acknowledgment changes on-call routing — understand implications
API keys are user-level — actions appear as that user in audit logs
Webhooks v3 vs v2 have different payload formats
Rate limits are per-account — shared across all integrations
Incidents have complex state machines — acknowledge before resolving
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. User-scoped API keys. OAuth2 for app-level. FedRAMP, SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA. Enterprise audit logging.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. User-scoped API keys. OAuth2. FedRAMP, SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA.
